rulings.Case Title: LEGALINK INC (FKA COMBS AND GREENLEY, INC) VS. JAY JACKMAN et al

rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday March 19, 2012, line 1. Plaintiff Combs And Greenley, Inc Notice Of Motion And Motion For Monetary Sanctions - Off calendar. This motion was heard on March 9, 2012. = 302 HK

rulings.Case Title: PROFESSIONAL COLLECTION CONSULTANTS VS. SUYAPA RODRIGUEZ et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SET ASIDE/VACATE JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON MONDAY, MARCH 19, 2012, LINE 1, DEFENDANT SUYAPA RODRIGUEZ MOTION FOR SET ASIDE/VACATE JUDGMENT. Denied. The motion was not filed within the time limits of either Code of Civil Procedure 473(b) or 473.5 and thus is untimely. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: MARK ROBINSON III VS. SAN FRANCISCO COMMUNITY COLLEGE DISTRICT et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Motion To File Records Under Seal
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON MONDAY, MARCH 19, 2012, LINE 15, DEFENDANT SAN FRANCISCO COMMUNITY COLLEGE DISTRICT, DON GRIFFIN, LAURIE SCOLARI Motion To File Records Under Seal. Denied in part. There has been an insufficient showing of need for confidentiality to overcome the right of public access to the records. The evidence previously designated by the parties as "confidential" is appropriate for public filing, and Defendants are permitted to file that evidence in support of their motion for summary judgment. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: KWANG HYUN KIM VS. CHE KYUNG PARK et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compelling Response To Special Interrogatories And Form Interrogatories, 2) Motion For Order That Matters
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday March 19, 2012, line 3. PLAINTIFF KWANG KIM Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compelling Response To Special Interrogatories And Form Interrogatories, 2) Motion For Order That Matters - Pro Tem Judge Katherine Huibonhoa, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Department 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Denied. Moving party failed to lodge courtesy copies as required by Local Rule 2.6B. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to katherinehuibonhoa@paulhastings.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests and referencing the case name and number in the subject line. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = 302/HK

rulings.Case Title: ALBINA FEDOROVA VS. KARIM CARY CANOUN MD et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON MONDAY, MARCH 19, 2012, LINE 4, DEFENDANT KARIM CANOUN MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Granted. Defendant satisified his burden of production negating the elements of breach and causation and plaintiff did not present any admissible evidence creating a triable issue on either of those elements. Defendant's objections to Dr. Korn's declaration are sustained. Dr. Korn's opinion lacks evidentiary support and the statements regarding lack of informed consent are irrelevant because that theory of recovery is not alleged in the complaint. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: PATRICK A MISSUD et al VS. D.R. HORTON, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Moiton To Allow Lab Investments, Llc To Intervene In This Action To Expunge Lis Pendens
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON MONDAY, MARCH 19, 2012, LINE 5, INTERVENOR LAB INVESTMENTS, LLC Motion To Allow Lab Investments, Llc To Intervene In This Action To Expunge Lis Pendens. Granted. LAB Investments, LLC is granted leave to intervene, the lis pendens is expunged and Mr. Missud must pay $4,030 to LAB Investments, LLC. LAB Investments, LLC has shown that it is the owner of the property, there is no real property claim concerning the property that has been filed in this action nor, even if there were, has Mr. Missud established the probable validity of such a claim. Mr. Missud's filing of the lis pendens and his refusal to withdraw it was without substantial justification. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: MINDY FREILE VS. TIM LINCECUM et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Noticeof Motion Andm Otion By Deft For A Stay Of Proceedings, Memo Of P&A; Declaration
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON MONDAY, MARCH 19, 2012, LINE 10, DEFENDANT TIM LINCECUM Motion For A Stay Of Proceedings. Denied. The arbitration clause is void per Civil Code 1953. Accordingly, neither the lessor nor the lessee can enforce it. PLAINTIFF LYUBOV LEBEDEVA Motion For Order Compelling The Depositions Of Rich Tran And City & County of San Francisco - Pro Tem Judge Katherine Huibonhoa, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Department 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion.
